loanAssumptions.rateMethod: Uses SBA variable-rate ceilings by loan size as a conservative estimate.

candidates.name: Accounting practice affluent NW suburb of Chicago, Semi-Absentee
candidates.notes: Established in 1987 with approximately 250 active clients, 6 full-time employees plus 2 part-time staff, and seller retirement explicitly disclosed. Monthly/quarterly billings plus bookkeeping and payroll create a meaningful recurring revenue base.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Long operating history, a sizable recurring client base, explicit retirement motivation, and a staffed delivery model make this one of the strongest accounting-platform fits in the current set.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: monthly and quarterly billing, bookkeeping, and payroll support durable repeat revenue and indicate the owner is not the whole operation.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Need to confirm client concentration, retention after transition, and how much rainmaking still depends on the seller despite the semi-absentee positioning.
financing_view: Preliminary view: business quality looks strong enough for serious lender interest, but the absence of a clear asking price means leverage and return profile still need to be underwritten from the CIM.
missing_facts: Asking price and implied multiple on SDE
Revenue mix across tax, bookkeeping, payroll, and advisory work
Top-client concentration and attrition history
Seller's exact weekly involvement in sales and technical review
Transition support and any seller carry

candidates.name: Growing IT & Web Hosting MSP w/ Recurring Revs $300K in SDE
candidates.notes: Managed IT, cloud, and web hosting provider established in 1989 with 3 employees, Microsoft and Dell partner credentials, seller financing availability, and explicit retirement motivation. Long-standing SMB and nonprofit client base suggests durable repeat revenue.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Recurring managed services and hosting revenue, long operating history, channel credentials, and explicit retirement motivation align well with the target profile for an owner-independent service business.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: the revenue base appears sticky and technical-service oriented, but diligence needs to confirm churn, contract term, and whether web hosting margins are sustainable.
underwriting_details.key_risks: The main concerns are customer concentration, dependence on the owner for technical escalation or sales, and whether the hosting layer introduces hidden infrastructure or support complexity.
financing_view: Preliminary view: cash flow coverage should support an SBA structure more comfortably if seller carry is real and the recurring revenue mix is validated.
missing_facts: MRR split across MSP, cloud, and hosting lines
Customer concentration and churn history
Owner role in sales, account management, and technical delivery
Infrastructure stack, vendor dependencies, and support obligations
Terms and size of any seller financing

candidates.name: CPA Practice - Hernando County Florida - $365K Revenue
candidates.notes: Tax, bookkeeping, and payroll mix. Listing notes a solid bookkeeper expected to continue.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Recurring tax, bookkeeping, and payroll revenue with long operating history and an apparent transition plan around a retained bookkeeper.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: strong fit on recurring service lines and cash flow level; price is reasonable relative to cash flow.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Seasonality, client concentration, and how much production still sits directly with the owner need validation.
financing_view: Preliminary view: likely SBA-serviceable if client retention and transferability hold up.
missing_facts: Is the seller retiring now, or just testing the market?
How many clients are payroll/monthly bookkeeping versus annual tax-only?
What percentage of revenue is tied to the top ten accounts?
What staff besides the bookkeeper support delivery?
Is there any seller carry available?

candidates.name: 26-Year Property Management & Concierge Service Business
candidates.notes: Serving approximately 20 homes with predictable recurring revenue, 3 employees, seller financing available, and retirement explicitly disclosed. Concierge model is relationship-driven but long operating history and repeat homeowner base fit the buy box.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: The listing shows long tenure, repeat homeowner relationships, disclosed cash flow, a small operating team, and verified retirement motivation with seller financing available.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: predictable management revenue is attractive, but the concierge component needs diligence to separate true recurring contracts from owner-driven service requests.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Relationship concentration is the main issue here because the business serves only about 20 homes in a premium market where a few account losses could materially hit earnings.
financing_view: Preliminary view: valuation versus cash flow is reasonable, and seller carry could make the deal more lender-friendly if retention and operational transferability check out.
missing_facts: Revenue split between management fees and concierge services
Client concentration and contract renewal terms
Exact responsibilities of the 3 employees
Owner role in homeowner relationships and emergency coordination
Seasonality and service-demand volatility by quarter

candidates.name: High Margin 24-Year Managed IT Services Provider
candidates.notes: 24-year operating history and solid cash flow. Recurring managed services profile.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Long operating history, strong disclosed cash flow, and recurring managed services make this one of the better listing-backed technical service targets.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: cash flow is attractive, but the asking price requires diligence on contract durability and owner dependence.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Need clarity on whether this is owner-heavy despite the strong margins, and whether the customer base is concentrated.
financing_view: Preliminary view: good business quality on paper, but SBA leverage may be tight at this price without seller carry.
missing_facts: Exact employee and contractor mix
Contract tenure and churn
Top-customer concentration
Owner role in sales and technical delivery
Seller carry and transition support

candidates.name: Fairfax County Full Service CPA & Bookkeeping
candidates.notes: Founded in 2008. Full-service CPA firm with accounting, financial statements, tax planning, and bookkeeping.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Full-service CPA and bookkeeping mix with meaningful cash flow, long history, and a service bundle that tends to support recurring relationships.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: attractive economics, but valuation is richer than the smaller practice listings.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Need to validate staffing depth, client concentration, and whether the asking price still makes sense after transition and retention risk.
financing_view: Preliminary view: likely financeable, but leverage will be heavier and diligence must support the higher price.
missing_facts: Exact staff count and tenure
Mix of bookkeeping, tax, and higher-value advisory work
Percentage of recurring monthly billings
Historical client retention
Seller transition terms and willingness to carry paper

candidates.name: Virtual Bookkeeping And Accounting Firm
candidates.notes: 100% recurring revenue with auto-renewing client contracts, 4 employees, owner no longer involved in day-to-day bookkeeping, and retirement disclosed. Remote delivery plus seasoned team make this one of the cleaner owner-independent fits.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: This is a strong fit on paper because the revenue is fully recurring, the service delivery is remote, the team is already in place, and the owner is reportedly out of day-to-day production with retirement disclosed.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: auto-renewing contracts and an owner-light operating model make the cash flow profile more defensible than most small bookkeeping listings.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Need to verify client retention economics, offshore or contractor dependencies if any, and whether the team can maintain service quality through ownership change without hidden owner intervention.
financing_view: Preliminary view: quality is attractive, but the valuation is full, so returns likely depend on contract durability, limited churn, and some combination of seller carry or downside price negotiation.
missing_facts: Client count, average account size, and concentration
Exact staffing model and tenure of the 4 employees
Scope of services by bookkeeping, controller, tax, and advisory lines
Churn history and renewal mechanics behind the auto-renewing contracts
Seller carry, transition support, and software stack transferability

candidates.name: Established Managed IT Services Provider with Recurring Revenue
candidates.notes: Recurring managed services provider with 3 contractors and documented operations.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Managed IT with explicit recurring revenue is a strong structural fit and this listing includes some operator depth via contractors.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: attractive business model, but price is high relative to current cash flow.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Valuation appears full, contractor reliance needs validation, and contract stickiness has to be proven.
financing_view: Preliminary view: may be financeable, but likely stretched on SBA debt service unless there is sizable seller carry or stronger normalized cash flow.
missing_facts: What percentage of revenue is on contract versus project work?
Are the contractors stable and transferable?
What is churn over the last 24 months?
Is there upside from add-on services not reflected in current cash flow?
Will the seller provide transition support or seller paper?

candidates.name: Consulting, Bookkeeping and Accounting Firm
candidates.notes: Bookkeeping and accounting listing with disclosed cash flow and established client base.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Recurring bookkeeping and accounting work is structurally attractive, and the disclosed cash flow falls inside the target buy box.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: price-to-cash-flow looks workable, but team depth and transferability need proof.
underwriting_details.key_risks: The main risk is whether this is really a team-backed client service firm versus an owner-heavy solo practice.
financing_view: Preliminary view: financing looks plausible, but people/process depth is the gating diligence item.
missing_facts: Who currently services the clients besides the owner?
What portion of revenue is recurring monthly versus advisory/project based?
Is seller transition support included?
What software stack and workflow documentation exist?
What is customer concentration?

candidates.name: COCoPropertyManagement
candidates.notes: SMB.co listing surfaced from Terrell Hills source page. Revenue/cash flow/valuation are present, but operating history and team depth still need validation.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Property management is attractive when doors under management and ops staff are real because revenue can be recurring and sticky.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: economics fit the buy box, but the SMB listing detail is thin and needs validation before moving higher.
underwriting_details.key_risks: The listing appears on an SMB city/source page with limited detail, so team depth, operating history, and actual portfolio quality are all unverified.
financing_view: Preliminary view: SBA payments should be manageable on paper, but confidence is lower until the business is validated directly.
missing_facts: Exact years in business
Doors under management
Team and maintenance/vendor structure
Customer concentration and churn
Seller motivation and transition support

candidates.name: GPRES Property Management
candidates.notes: SMB.co listing surfaced from Oakmont source page. Revenue/cash flow/valuation are present, but operating history and team depth still need validation.

underwriting_details.why_it_fits: Property management can fit the recurring-revenue buy box if the managed portfolio and operating system are legitimate and transferable.
revenue_and_cash_flow_view: Initial take: SMB snippet shows workable revenue, cash flow, and valuation, but detail depth is materially lower than broker listings.
underwriting_details.key_risks: Listing detail is sparse, so portfolio size, staffing, and actual transferability are unclear.
financing_view: Preliminary view: financeable on paper, but diligence confidence is lower until source details are expanded.
missing_facts: Exact years in business
Doors under management
Team structure
Portfolio retention and churn
Seller motivation and transition terms
